steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The passage pivots from Cunningham's individual story to Black journalism's broader history: "Cunningham's accomplishment is just one example of the rich history of Black journalism in the United States." That pivot sentence is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The National Association of Black Journalists was founded in Washington, DC, in the 1900s.': Misrepresents a supporting detail (the NABJ was founded in 1975, which is in the 1900s, but this cherry-picks one fact and ignores the main idea about Black journalism's history). ✗
  • Choice B — 'Black journalists has an interesting history both inside and outside the United States.': Expands scope beyond what the passage covers — the passage is limited to the United States and never mentions other countries. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Cunningham's achievements are often overlooked in countries other than the United States.': Introduces an unsupported claim; the passage never states Cunningham is overlooked anywhere. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Black journalism in the United States has an important history that the National Association of Black Journalists helps to honor.': Captures both the central claim (rich history of Black journalism in the US) and the supporting role of the NABJ, matching the passage's main point. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
